statement.en: Dissecting the conformational equilibrium of Src kinase using single-molecule FRET
statement.summary: Kinases are responsible for the posttranslational phosphorylation of amino acid residues. Src non-receptor tyrosine kinases belong to the human kinase family. Src and Src family kinases were intensively investigated due to their malignant transformation and oncogenesis characters. Published crystal structures and biochemical studies indicate that Src has open and close conformation. And phosphorylation sites Y419 and Y530 are essential regulation sites responsible for the conformational change and kinase activity of Src. However, a quantitative analysis of Src regulation and conformation equilibrium has still not been conducted.\r Here we set about to provide detailed kinetic analysis and insight into conformation equilibrium for Src. As a starting point, I did ATP-NADH coupled enzymatic a

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper describes experimental methodology (single-molecule FRET) applied to Src kinase conformational dynamics. Abstract is incomplete (cuts mid-sentence at 'enzymatic a'), preventing full evaluation. The stated research objective—quantitative kinetic analysis of Src conformational equilibrium—is scientifically motivated and addresses a gap in literature, but results/validation are not presented in the provided excerpt. Classification as FLOWING reflects: (1) preprint status with incomplete abstract, (2) provisional experimental design not yet yielding validated conclusions, (3) typical trajectory of structural biology manuscripts under peer review.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: リン酸化が開閉の分岐点を指図し、タンパク質の呼吸は自由エネルギーの勾配に従う。
